questions.question: How does a Python virtual environment work under the hood?
questions.answer: A virtual environment is a lightweight folder containing copies/symlinks of the Python executable and pip. Activating it prepends the `.venv/bin` directory to the shell's `PATH` environment variable. When you type `python` or `pip`, the OS finds the `.venv` executables first, isolating package installations to `.venv/lib/python3.x/site-packages`.
